steps.title: Look for one variable value
steps.content: A linear equation has exactly one solution when simplifying leaves one specific value for the variable. If simplifying gives a false statement, there is no solution; if it gives an identity, there are infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Solve the candidate equation
steps.content: The equation $$ \frac{1}{36}+x=-x-\frac{1}{36} $$ leaves a single variable value. $$ \frac{1}{36}+2x=-\frac{1}{36} $$ $$ 2x=-\frac{2}{36} $$ $$ x=-\frac{1}{36} $$ The answer is B.
